expectations.text: Tells the user to delete (or remove) the hand-written FormSubmissionIntegrationController
expectations.evidence: Delete the hand-written `FormSubmissionIntegrationController` entirely ... Delete `FormSubmissionIntegrationController` entirely. No controller file. None.

expectations.text: Explains that ABP wires the integration-service HTTP exposure implicitly (via the integration-services pipeline / /integration-api / ExposeIntegrationServices / ApplicationServiceTypes.IntegrationServices), so writing a controller is unnecessary or wrong
expectations.evidence: ABP's **Auto API Controllers** machinery already exposes application services (including integration services) over HTTP **implicitly** ... `options.ExposeIntegrationServices = true;`

expectations.text: Confirms that the [IntegrationService] interface and its ApplicationService implementation (the first two blocks) are correct as written
expectations.evidence: Contracts project — interface ... Correct: `[IntegrationService]` attribute applied, `IApplicationService` base, placed in the `*.Application.Contracts` project ... Application project — implementation ... Also correct

expectations.text: Calls out that integration services are not meant to live on the public /api surface (any of: hidden from public API, audit-off, no [Authorize] by default — i.e. mismatched with what the controller is doing)
expectations.evidence: Hidden from the public `/api/...` Swagger / discovery surface ... Audit-logging off by default ... No `[Authorize]` applied by default ... Slapping `[Authorize]` on your controller imposes UI-style user authentication
